text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY — URGENT MULTIDISCIPLINARY REASSESSMENT NOTE

ATTENDING: [Thoracic Medical Oncology]
CC: Radiation Oncology, Neurosurgery, Thoracic Surgery

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT
Urgent reassessment following CT evidence of systemic progression and new brain metastases after 14 months on osimertinib (Tagrisso) for stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma. Tissue biopsy of new right adrenal metastasis and repeat NGS performed; results now available and reviewed with multidisciplinary team.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Tran is a 63-year-old Vietnamese-American female with a history of stage IIIB (T3N2M0) right lower lobe lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant, diagnosed approximately 14 months ago. She was initiated on osimertinib 80 mg daily as first-line therapy given the EGFR-activating mutation, a shared decision made in the context of a multidisciplinary tumor board favoring upfront targeted therapy over concurrent chemoradiation given her comorbidities and performance status. She achieved a significant and durable partial response on Tagrisso, with the primary tumor decreasing from 4.8 cm to 1.8 cm and complete radiographic resolution of mediastinal adenopathy by the 9-month mark.

Her most recent restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (obtained approximately 5 weeks ago at the 14-month mark) demonstrated alarming new findings: enlargement of the right lower lobe primary from 1.8 cm back to 3.1 cm, a new 1.4 cm right adrenal nodule, multiple new small bilateral pulmonary nodules (largest 8 mm, right upper lobe), and recurrent right paratracheal (4R) adenopathy measuring 1.2 cm. Concurrent urgent MRI brain revealed three new enhancing intracranial lesions: a 1.1 cm right cerebellar lesion with surrounding edema, a 0.8 cm left frontal lesion, and a 0.6 cm right parietal lesion. No leptomeningeal enhancement was identified, and there is no mass effect requiring urgent neurosurgical intervention.

CT-guided biopsy of the right adrenal nodule was subsequently performed and confirmed metastatic lung adenocarcinoma (TTF-1 positive). Tissue was sent for comprehensive NGS (523-gene panel, tumor-normal paired analysis). Results are now finalized and reviewed today: the dominant resistance mechanism is an acquired EGFR T790M mutation (allele frequency 19.4%) alongside retention of the original L858R (allele frequency 28.1%). EGFR C797S was NOT detected. Subclonal PIK3CA E545K (allele frequency 8.3%) and low-level CDK4 amplification were identified as potential co-contributors to resistance. MET copy number was 3.8 (borderline, not high-level amplification). Liquid biopsy (plasma ctDNA) was concordant with tissue findings. Overall: T790M-dominant acquired resistance without an actionable bypass track.

Today she presents for multidisciplinary review of these results and definitive treatment planning. She denies any new neurological symptoms — no headaches, no focal weakness, no seizures, no vision changes, no gait instability. She does note worsening exertional dyspnea over the past 4–5 weeks, reduced from her prior baseline response state. She reports a 1.5 kg weight loss. She remains active and is still attending work part-time. No fevers, chills, or hemoptysis.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
- Constitutional: Fatigue, mild (grade 1). Weight loss ~1.5 kg over 5 weeks. No night sweats.
- Respiratory: Worsening dyspnea with exertion, no rest dyspnea, no hemoptysis, no pleuritic pain.
- Neurological: DENIES headache, focal weakness, numbness, visual changes, ataxia, seizures, or cognitive changes.
- GI: Mild nausea (grade 1), intermittent. Bowel habits normal. No diarrhea currently.
- Skin: Residual grade 1 xerosis from prior osimertinib-related rash, otherwise improved.
- MSK: No bone pain. No back pain.
- All other systems reviewed and negative.

---

PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY
- Stage IIIB (T3N2M0) right lower lobe lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant, TTF-1+/NapsinA+, PD-L1 TPS 15% (22C3), TMB 6.2 mut/Mb (low), MSS. Diagnosed ~14 months ago.
- First-line therapy: Osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily; best response partial response (primary reduced ~63% at nadir); duration of response ~14 months until current progression.
- EGFR T790M acquired resistance mutation identified on adrenal biopsy NGS.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
- Hypertension, well-controlled (amlodipine 5 mg daily)
- Hypothyroidism, well-controlled (levothyroxine 88 mcg daily)
- Former smoker, 12 pack-years, quit ~19 years ago

SURGICAL HISTORY
- CT-guided transthoracic needle biopsy of right lower lobe (initial diagnosis)
- CT-guided biopsy of right adrenal nodule (resistance evaluation)

ALLERGIES
- No known drug allergies

MEDICATIONS
- Osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg orally daily [PENDING DISCONTINUATION per today's plan]
- Levothyroxine 88 mcg orally daily
- Amlodipine 5 mg orally daily
- Topical clindamycin 1% gel to face/neck PRN (residual osimertinib rash)

SOCIAL HISTORY
- Married, two adult children. Works as a high school biology teacher (part-time currently). Vietnamese-American, immigrated at age 14. Lives locally with family support. Non-smoker (former, quit ~19 years ago). No alcohol or recreational drug use. Good social support network. Highly educated regarding her diagnosis.

FAMILY HISTORY
- No known family history of lung cancer or EGFR-driven malignancies. Mother with breast cancer at age 72. Father with gastric cancer, age 68.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vitals: BP 124/76, HR 78 bpm, RR 16, SpO2 95% on room air, Temp 36.7°C, Weight 58.2 kg
ECOG Performance Status: 1

General: Alert and oriented x3. Well-appearing female in no acute distress. Conversational without respiratory difficulty at rest.
HEENT: No papilledema on fundoscopic exam (performed by neurology). Pupils equal and reactive. No lymphadenopathy in the cervical or supraclavicular chains.
Neurological: Cranial nerves II–XII intact. Motor strength 5/5 in all four extremities. Sensation intact to light touch and proprioception. Cerebellar finger-nose-finger intact bilaterally; tandem gait without ataxia. Gait normal. No pronator drift.
Respiratory: Breath sounds reduced at the right base. No wheeze. Mild dullness to percussion right base. No crackles.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. No JVD.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender. No organomegaly palpable. No palpable right upper quadrant or flank mass.
Skin: Grade 1 xerosis of the face and neck. No active papulopustular rash. Mild periungual changes from prior paronychia, resolved.
Extremities: No edema. No calf tenderness.

---

LAB RESULTS (REVIEWED TODAY)
- CBC: WBC 7.4 k/uL, Hgb 11.2 g/dL (mild normocytic anemia, stable), Plt 198 k/uL
- CMP: Sodium 138, Potassium 4.1, Creatinine 0.89 mg/dL (CrCl ~68 mL/min), Total bili 0.7, ALT 22, AST 18, Albumin 3.8
- LFTs within normal limits
- TSH: 2.9 (well-controlled on current levothyroxine dose)
- LDH: 187 U/L (mildly elevated)
- EKG: Normal sinus rhythm, QTc 428 ms
- NGS adrenal biopsy: EGFR L858R retained + EGFR T790M acquired; EGFR C797S absent; subclonal PIK3CA E545K; low-level CDK4 amp; no high-level MET amp; no ERBB2 amp; no fusion events. Full report reviewed.

---

IMAGING RESULTS (REVIEWED TODAY)
- CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (approximately 5 weeks ago): RLL primary enlarging to 3.1 cm from 1.8 cm nadir; new bilateral pulmonary nodules (largest 8 mm RUL); new 1.4 cm right adrenal nodule; recurrent 4R adenopathy 1.2 cm. No pleural effusion.
- MRI brain with/without gadolinium: Three new enhancing lesions — right cerebellum 1.1 cm (peritumoral edema present), left frontal 0.8 cm, right parietal 0.6 cm. No leptomeningeal enhancement. No hydrocephalus or mass effect requiring emergent intervention.
- Right adrenal biopsy pathology: Metastatic adenocarcinoma, TTF-1+, consistent with lung primary.

---

ASSESSMENT
Ms. Tran is a 63-year-old woman with stage IV (now M1b/M1c — adrenal and brain metastases) EGFR L858R-mutant right lower lobe lung adenocarcinoma with documented radiographic and pathological progression after 14 months of first-line osimertinib. The dominant acquired resistance mechanism is EGFR T790M (confirmed on adrenal metastasis NGS and concordant with plasma ctDNA), without evidence of an actionable bypass track (no high-level MET amplification, no ERBB2 amplification, no new fusion event). EGFR C797S — which would be the expected further resistance mutation had she been rechallenged with a third-gen TKI — is absent, consistent with early evolution. Subclonal PIK3CA E545K and CDK4 amplification are of uncertain clinical significance at this time.

The brain metastases are asymptomatic but require treatment; given only three discrete lesions, favorable performance status (ECOG 1), and the absence of leptomeningeal disease, stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) is strongly preferred over whole-brain radiation therapy (WBRT). Systemic progression is confirmed and requires a change in systemic therapy. Platinum-doublet chemotherapy (carboplatin + pemetrexed) is the appropriate next systemic line for this EGFR-mutant patient after third-generation TKI failure, with a reasonable expected response in adenocarcinoma histology. Clinical trial options for post-osimertinib T790M-bearing NSCLC were discussed with the patient and family at length; no immediately available suitable trial is open at this institution at present, but referrals will be made to regional centers.

This case was reviewed today at multidisciplinary tumor board with input from radiation oncology, neurosurgery, and thoracic medical oncology. All parties are in agreement with the management plan below.

---

PLAN

1. BRAIN METASTASES — SRS:
   - Radiation oncology has been consulted and concurs with Gamma Knife stereotactic radiosurgery for all three intracranial lesions. WBRT deferred given limited number of metastases, intact cognition, and desire to preserve neurocognitive function.
   - SRS planning to proceed within the week. Neurosurgery has evaluated and no surgical resection indicated at this time.
   - Osimertinib will be CONTINUED through SRS delivery and then discontinued immediately after the final SRS fraction is completed.
   - Dexamethasone 4 mg BID initiated today for perilesional edema management, with planned taper following SRS.

2. SYSTEMIC THERAPY — SECOND LINE:
   - Following completion of SRS, osimertinib will be discontinued.
   - Initiate carboplatin AUC 5 IV day 1 + pemetrexed (Alimta) 500 mg/m2 IV day 1, every 21 days.
   - Pemetrexed supplementation: folic acid 400 mcg daily to begin immediately (at least 7 days before cycle 1 pemetrexed); vitamin B12 1000 mcg IM to be given this week and repeated every 9 weeks; dexamethasone 4 mg BID the day before, day of, and day after pemetrexed to minimize cutaneous toxicity.
   - Patient BSA 1.67 m2 (pemetrexed dose ~835 mg; carboplatin per Calvert formula to AUC 5 based on CrCl 68 mL/min).
   - Baseline CBC, CMP, LFTs, and EKG reviewed today — acceptable for chemotherapy initiation.
   - Antiemetics: ondansetron 8 mg prior to and PRN; lorazepam PRN for delayed nausea.

3. CLINICAL TRIALS:
   - Patient and family are strongly interested in clinical trial participation. T790M-bearing EGFR-mutant post-third-gen TKI population is an active area of trial development. Will refer to regional academic center (UCSD/Stanford thoracic oncology programs) for trial availability review. Will continue to monitor Clinicaltrials.gov for institutional and cooperative group trials.
   - KRASG12C-independent RAS pathway inhibition and EGFR/MET bispecific strategies (e.g., amivantamab-based) are emerging options relevant to this patient's molecular profile and will be flagged for future enrollment consideration.

4. MONITORING — CNS:
   - Post-SRS MRI brain to be obtained approximately 8–10 weeks after radiosurgery to assess treatment response.
   - Neurological exam and symptom assessment at each visit.
   - Seizure precautions discussed; no prophylactic anticonvulsants indicated at this time per neurology.

5. SUPPORTIVE CARE:
   - Continue dexamethasone per SRS protocol, then taper. Monitor blood glucose closely given steroid use (patient has no baseline diabetes, but will screen).
   - Continue levothyroxine 88 mcg daily, amlodipine 5 mg daily.
   - Nutritional assessment — mild weight loss noted; refer to oncology dietitian.
   - Social work referral offered; patient and husband declined at this time but remain open.
   - Palliative care co-management offered — patient and family prefer to defer formal palliative care consultation at this time but understand the door remains open.

6. FOLLOW-UP:
   - Follow-up in clinic approximately 3 weeks after SRS for cycle 1 chemotherapy evaluation.
   - Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis planned after 3 cycles of carboplatin/pemetrexed (~9–10 weeks into chemotherapy).

The patient and her husband were present for the full discussion. All findings, resistance mechanisms, and treatment options were explained in detail using lay terminology. Questions were answered thoroughly. The patient expressed understanding and agreement with the outlined plan. She was notably composed and expressed gratitude for the thorough explanation. She understands this represents a significant transition in her disease course.

Time spent: approximately 65 minutes total (including multidisciplinary conference time and patient counseling).

Electronically signed,
[Attending, Thoracic Medical Oncology]
[Fellow, Hematology/Oncology]
